Crime drama, starring David Caruso and Emily Procter(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bank at Quantico is robbed and a security guard killed, but the team soon discovers that stealing money may not have been the perpetrators' primary objective. Meanwhile, Gibbs is given the task of evaluating an inexperienced new agent assigned by Vance to help investigate the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rey Valentin(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones(n)

programme.title: Matlock

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is discovered during a city-wide power cut - and because Jethro and the team are unable to use their modern electronic equipment, they have to solve the case the old-fashioned way, and it soon becomes apparent that national security could be at stake. Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker and his team get caught in a deadly factional argument when an IRA member's daughter is kidnapped by the rival of a terrorist splinter group with a grudge against her father(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Crime drama spin-off, starring Patricia Arquette as Special Agent Avery Ryan, head of the FBI's cyber crime division. The team's first assignment involves a kidnapped baby and as Ryan and forensics expert Elijah Mundo investigate the clues they discover that whoever was responsible hacked into a baby monitor to establish when the child was alone and vulnerable. Also starring James Van Der Beek(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate a government organisation dedicated to child prodigies while researching the death of a marine whose body appeared in the middle of a field disguised as a scarecrow, and realise a 12-year-old could be the killer's next target. programme.desc: The chief inspector comes up against Rattigan, an old adversary from his days in London, whose legal challenge to his prison sentence has been upheld on the grounds that Gently fabricated evidence against him. programme.desc: Part one of two. Gerry is spooked by the discovery of UCOS's latest victim - a cop, whose body has lain untouched under a basement for 30 years. As accusations of police corruption and underhand dealings begin to surface, Gerry has to face up to some skeletons in his own closet and it's a race against time for the team to help before his murky past catches up with him. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bex's concerns for Louise leave Lisa worried and she is more determined than ever to help her daughter. But as Louise opens up, the guilt becomes too much and she makes a shocking confession. Mick forces Linda to face the consequences of her actions, Billy shares his worries about Honey with Lola and Jean enlists Habiba's help to celebrate Kat's birthday. Ben is shocked when Callum arrives at the car lot, while Martin receives good news - the hit-and-run victim is awake(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a body is found in a field near the village of Hartford, Nikki and the Lyell team confirm it is that of missing teenager Jason Forbes. Working to the theory that most killers strike close to home, ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect and sets her sights firmly on local locksmith Malcolm Wilde. Forensic crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern, David Caves and Liz Carr, with Sophia Di Martino, Robert Pugh and Phil Davis(n)

programme.title: Ladhood

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa is convinced Louise is unwell after saying what she did about Keanu, and when Sharon gets wind of her stepdaughter's confession, she is convinced it cannot be true. Phil, however, tells a different story. Linda hides her hangover from Mick and feels the pressure at work with Shirley watching her every move, Sonia finds herself in an impossible situation and Billy goes to extreme lengths to help Honey. Rainie tries to help Callum start dating again and Kat is furious to learn Leo sabotaged her plan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Tottenham Hotspur v Middlesbrough (Kick-off 8.05pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the match from Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as the sides compete for the right to face Southampton away in the next round. Championship outfit Boro took the lead in the reverse fixture through Ashley Fletcher, but Lucas Moura equalised for the Londoners and Jose Mourinho's side will now expect to prevail in front of their own fans(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: A bodybuilder joins the team to help promote the health benefits of a vegan diet, persuading the regulars at local gyms that they do not need animal protein to build up their muscles. The others opt for more aggressive measures by staging a vigil at a slaughterhouse, which leads to a counter-protest from farmers, who feel their livelihood is being threatened(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part one of two. An analysis of her DNA leads Nadiya Hussain to a surprising discovery that sees her travel to far-flung places to explore the people, places and food there. The former Great British Bake Off winner begins by visiting Cambodia, where she uncovers a bakery school training up girls from tough backgrounds in the art of French patisserie, before heading to Thailand, where she learns how to make a curry paste unique to the Muslim community on the tiny island of Koh Sukhorn(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

With pressure building on the police to secure a conviction, the team must fight to ensure that inconvenient truths are not overlooked(n)

programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited

However, judges Monica Galetti and Marcus Wareing will ultimately decide who deserves a place in Knockout Week(n)

With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers unearth hundreds of years of fascinating history hidden in Britain's public houses, beginning in Yorkshire. They visit an establishment believed to have been drunk dry by troops the night before the Battle of Marston Moor during the English Civil War, while in York they learn about a pub that once doubled up as a hospital and has a gruesome history.(n)

programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the West Country between the wars, setting off from the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives and ending his first in the former mining village of St Day(n)

programme.desc: Doctors Chris and Xand van Tulleken test the best ways to beat fatigue with the help of pairs of identical twins, with Tina and Hugo trying out popular sleep regimens to find out if there is a way to combat exhaustion. Elsewhere, the participants also take part in experiments where they learn real-world skills in a virtual world, and test new theory about smart phones and the effect that their use can have on IQs(n)

Both teams have won one game and lost another, so this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set consisting of The Whitsun Weddings, Homeward Bound, Oh Dr Beeching!, and Brief Encounter(n)

programme.title: Exposed: The Church's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of the decades-long pursuit of former bishop Peter Ball by those brave individuals determined to bring him to justice for sexual abuse, and the cover-up that went to the highest levels of the Church of England. Using powerful testimony from victims, police and church officials, and dramatic reconstruction, this programme charts the story of those who fought for many years to bring a prosecution against Ball(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Edwyn Collins reflects on the year in which he had a worldwide hit with A Girl Like You, while Mike Flowers lost out on the Christmas number one spot with his lounge cover of Oasis hit Wonderwall. Alcopops became the fashionable drink despite a storm of controversy, Braveheart and The Usual Suspects pulled in cinema audiences, and Damien Hirst touched a nerve in the art world(n)

programme.desc: As the tenth week of the competition begins, Lord sugar summons the candidates to Burlington House in Central London. Here, the remaining candidates are informed that for this week's task, they have been challenged to create and sell their own perfume, coming up with their own signature scent and design to sell to major retailers. For one team a failure to make decisions proves problematic, while on the other team a bold attempt to make their fragrance stand out does not go to plan. Back in the boardroom, one of the candidates is sent home from the competition(n)

programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show(n)

With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Dave takes Si on a pub tour of the Lake District, following in the footsteps of the poets Wordsworth and Coleridge. They sample negus, a wine-based drink from the late 1800s, and take part in a traditional pub competition to find out who is 'The Biggest Liar'(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Jonathan Pratt and Michael Baggott assess items at Barrow Hill Roundhouse in Derbyshire and Paul Martin visits the village of Hathersage, which provided inspiration for Jane Eyre(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Cornwall's county town, Truro, heading to the historic estate of Trewithen, whose gardens were stocked from China by professional plant hunters commissioned by its owner. The Atlantic Coast branch line carries Michael north to Newquay, where he discovers a pioneering surfer and braves the waves on a belly board. In the nearby village of St Mawgan, Michael is introduced to the ancient Cornish sport of 'wrassling', which surged in popularity between the wars as part of a Cornish Celtic revival(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the families emerge from the first few hectic weeks with their new babies and realise that they need to re-evaluate their priorities. Paul and Craig strive to be truly equal parents, and Eilidh and Richard wonder whether they will ever get to take their baby home. Meanwhile, Mel returns to see the twins(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Competition is fierce as the ring-netters of Newlyn go on the hunt for sardines. Also known as pilchards, they were once a staple of the Cornish fishing industry, caught using drift nets by the tens-of-thousands, then packed in salt and sent to Italy. But demand waned and the drift net fishery, once the mainstay of the Cornish fleet, faded away in the 1960s. In recent years, the humble pilchard has been rebranded as the Cornish sardine, and the hundreds of drift netters have been replaced by just 15 hi-tech ring-netters - and the sardine is once more a highly-prized and lucrative catch(n)

programme.title: Exposed: The Church's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the survivors of Peter Ball's abuse, and the safeguarding officers who came together to pursue the former bishop until his eventual conviction and imprisonment. The programme reveals how Church money was used to hire a private investigator to discredit Ball's victims, though this report was buried when it honestly reported that Ball was in fact a prolific abuser. On finally discovering the report, one of the safeguarding officers handed it to the police, who decided that Peter Ball was worthy of investigation(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: After a hard day's work in a monster movie in the desert, Sam takes Duke to a science evening at her school, where she finds it hard to keep her temper(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedy performers Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson, also known as Two Episodes of Mash, launch a series of spoofs by parodying a 1970s documentary about Luton Library(n)

programme.desc: Comedians Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson parody archive documentaries from the 1960s, examining what telecommunications in the 1990s might be like(n)

The film shows Dot as she tries to balance a burgeoning green holiday destination with the demands of a busy working farm(n)

programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lee Price has dreamt of living in Australia ever since getting married there, so he, his wife Amanda and their children head Down Under for a trial week. Nicki Chapman presents(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam goes on ‘Staycation' in the North of England. Beginning his adventure at a living museum, he achieves his dream of driving a steam train. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley reveal how fake car insurance is leaving thousands of people driving uninsured on Britain's roads without their knowledge. Plus, the health supplements that are totally useless, containing no active ingredients, the children's toys that contain gender-altering chemicals and tips on recognising fake modern collectibles(n)

programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Live Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Kyren Wilson v Jack Lisowski. Coverage of the first-round match on the fourth day at Alexandra Palace in London. Wilson was a runner-up in 2018, while Lisowski made his debut in the competition last year, when he was defeated by Ding Junhui at this stage. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Pubs That Built Britain
programme.desc: Si and Dave take a Manchester pub crawl to uncover historic establishments once at the heart of the city's industrial revolution. Starting in Middleton, they discover that politics, protest and plotting were rife in a pub's back rooms in the early 1800s, before visiting a local brewery to find out more about the popularity of beer in Britain(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Christina Trevanion assess items brought into Allerton Castle, North Yorkshire, including two Moorcroft vases and a ruby necklace(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andrea McLean, Adil Ray, Rita Simons and Phil Wang test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches Bodmin en route to Totnes, as he explores the West Country from St Ives to Salisbury Plain. Out on the rugged moor, Michael hears how the celebrated author Daphne du Maurier captivated readers between the wars with her tales of smuggling at the Jamaica Inn. In Devon, Michael takes the plunge at Plymouth's beautiful art deco Tinside Lido, while at Ivybridge, he boards a vehicle like no other to cross the causeway to Burgh Island, where a 1930s playboy built a splendid art deco party palace(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: As Storm Ophelia hits the British Isles, on the south east coast of Ireland the pagers of the Rosslare crew go off, to attend to a small yacht with three people on board that is caught in one of the worst storms in living memory. In Devon, the crew at Appledore race an incoming tide to try to reach a man stuck fast in quicksand, while on the north coast of Cornwall, the crew of St Agnes must battle through huge breaking waves to reach two young boys being swept out to sea in a deadly rip current(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley and a team find out if eating less food can boost your memory, and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim investigates over-the-counter cannabis-based health products. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals how to spot a hidden illness that can cause stillbirth, and Alain Gregoire discovers the body's secret built-in stress reliever(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: It's the first full week of the challenge and Tom is back in Gloucester, the city where he grew up, to exercise and cook alongside his diet and fitness gang. As team chef, he wants to get them off to a good start on calorie counting - and familiar, comforting dishes is the way to go. Window cleaning brothers-in-law Dan and Alex volunteered for the challenge because of health concerns and a normal day sees them tucking into a big, calorific breakfast and grabbing fast food between jobs, but Tom joins the boys on a bike ride in the local countryside before giving them a tasty breakfast muffin to try(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Angel Aziraphale and demon Crowley are enjoying their lives on Earth and have become firm friends, but their comfortable existence is disrupted when Crowley is summoned by two demons, Hastur and Ligur, and presented with a newborn baby who is the Antichrist. The two friends vow to collaborate to ensure the boy will not bring about the end of the world. Neil Gaiman's adaptation of his and Terry Pratchett's fantasy adventure, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Terry Pratchett: Back in Black
programme.desc: A dramatised documentary telling the story of the life of Terry Pratchett, the fantasy author whose popular Discworld series made him a household name, selling more then 85 million copies worldwide. In 2015, following a prolonged and very public battle with Alzheimers, Terry died, leaving behind one last unfinished story - his own. Here, actor Paul Kaye steps into the celebrated writer's shoes, charting Terry's life from his troubled schooldays and his difficulties securing the respect of literary critics, through to his eventual success, and his gradual decline into ill health. The film makes use of Terry's own words, along with those of his friend and collaborator Neil Gaiman, contemporary Val McDermid, and long-serving assistant Rob Wilkins(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Snooker: The Masters
programme.desc: Mark Williams v Stuart Bingham. Action from the first-round match on the fourth day at Alexandra Palace in London. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, John Parrott, Ken Doherty, Dennis Taylor, Stephen Hendry and John Virgo(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the craftspeople behind the manufacture, maintenance and restoration of Steinway pianos, from the German factory workers bending the frames and polishing the veneers to longstanding British restorer Jeff about to retire from the company. Steinway supplies pianos to the royal household as well as many leading performers, and the film goes behind the scenes as renowned pianist Lang Lang prepares for a concert at the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Documentary examining Peter Sutcliffe's crimes and asking whether 1970s attitudes to women influenced the investigation, leaving the murderer free to kill again. The first episode explores the early years of the investigation from 1975, when the first Ripper murder occurred. The location of the first two murders in Chapeltown, then well-known as Leeds' main red light district, led the police to decide that prostitution was the connection between the attacks. After the second murder in January 1976, the police announced they were hunting a 'prostitute killer' and the investigation became driven by this theory(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling(n)

programme.title: The Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munch's The Scream(n)

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: Eamonn McCabe explores how photographers responded to historic events they witnessed in the first half of the 20th century, including the two world wars and the Great Depression(n)

programme.title: Paula Rego: Secrets and Stories
programme.desc: Making use of new interviews and a vast archive of home video, film-maker Nick Willing compiles an intimate portrait of his mother, the celebrated painter Paula Rego. While Rego has been notoriously private and guarded about her personal life, she opens up to her son, discussing secrets and stories from her live, which has seen her battle fascism, misogyny in art world, and manic depression. Willing's film dictates his mother's story from her native Portugal, where she used her pictures as a weapon against dictatorship, through to settling in London, where she turned her craft toward tackling women's issues such as abortion rights(n)

programme.title: Handmade: By Royal Appointment
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the craftspeople behind the manufacture, maintenance and restoration of Steinway pianos, from the German factory workers bending the frames and polishing the veneers to longstanding British restorer Jeff about to retire from the company. Steinway supplies pianos to the royal household as well as many leading performers, and the film goes behind the scenes as renowned pianist Lang Lang prepares for a concert at the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon meets the owner of a traditional wooden house in Barbados who has turned down millions of dollars from luxury property developers, then encounters marijuana growers on the volcanic island of St Vincent hoping that the drug will soon be decriminalised. On the South American mainland, Simon examines the mismanagement of Venezuela's natural resources, before crossing into Colombia, where he spends time with the Kogi - an indigenous people who have maintained their traditional forest lifestyle(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Wales and discover a strong industrial engineering history, sailing across Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, the highest navigable aqueduct in the world. The tiny village of Hawarden gives the duo the opportunity to stay overnight in the UK's only residential library, before they make an attempt on the summit of Mount Snowdon itself - via the mountain's iconic rack-and-pinion narrow gauge steam railway train(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venice's beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how promising lines in the Yorkshire Ripper inquiry were overlooked as a hoax tape took centre stage(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Surgeon Gabriel Weston explores cases that were cracked by examining the smallest traces of forensic evidence. They include the first murder case solved in the UK based on fingerprint evidence to the patterns of blood in a bedroom which helped overturn an infamous murder conviction. Gabriel also reveals the cutting-edge techniques that are proving vital to catch the killers of today(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank explores Britain's love affair with the terrace, charting the highs of Victorian industry, the lows of their post-war decline and on to their 21st-century regeneration - which for one street in Toxteth, Liverpool, even saw them awarded the 2015 Turner Prize(n)

programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines the three 'golden ages' of British sculpture and the technical breakthroughs that made them possible. He begins with a look at the Norman ecclesiastical building programme and the masterworks created by anonymous medieval masons and artisans to the glory of God - until Henry VIII's Reformation brought wholesale destruction(n)

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the art of the new cities of the east coast of America, with their futuristic skylines and lofty skyscrapers. He explores the squalid boxing rings painted by George Bellows, Reginald Mash's decadent away days on Coney Island and the escape into theosophy and abstraction mounted by Thomas Wilfred. He ends by looking at the harsh immigrant experience of Ellis Island and the profound impact that rootlessness had on the art of Mark Rothko.(n)

programme.title: The Mystery of Van Gogh's Ear
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman joins forces with 'art sleuth' Bernadette Murphy in a bid to solve one of the greatest and bloodiest mysteries of the art world - why did Vincent Van Gogh take a blade to his own ear in December, 1888? The pair set out to discover why the Dutch post-impressionist chose to mutilate himself, and also to identify the unknown girl to whom he is reported to have given is severed ear. Their quest takes them from Vincent's former home in the south of France to the Van Gogh Museum in Amsterdam, and along the way, they seek to determine the causes of the painter's debilitating mental turmoil. The answers they are looking for, however, might not lie in Europe. Instead, a library in California may hold crucial evidence that - if verified - could cause an art world sensation(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott explore the Italian city of Venice, using 3D technology to discover how a city built in a swamp came to be one of the most powerful locations in Medieval Europe. The pair dive deep into the many canals in this unique place to experience how its buildings still remain standing to this day, and highlight how Venice's beauty once masked a ruthless secret state, and a world of excess and vice(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: James is faced with reassembling a 1970s Honda Z50A Mini Trail Motorcycle from all its 303 component parts. This exciting, portable motorcycle was fun for the whole family and had a generation of young people hooked on motorcycles for the rest of their lives(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Following the astonishing wildlife, landscapes and people of the world's longest river, the Nile, beginning in the tropical mountain range of the Rwenzoris, home to one of the world's strangest birds, the shoebill stork. Then, cameras follow the Nile's journey as it travels through the wild Savannah lands of Uganda, before reaching the Ethiopian highlands where it receives an injection of water far to the East(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke looks at art from one of Ancient Egypt's most opulent eras, beginning with the troubling psychological portraits of tyrant Senwosret III, who ruled during the Middle Kingdom, through to the golden mask of Tutankhamun, which was created hundreds of years later. He also explores architectural wonders, exquisite tombs and a lost city, where an artistic revolution took place and a new creative style was developed under Akhenaten and Nefertiti(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: A short stint as a carer opened Hayley Pearce's eyes to the devastating effects of dementia, failing health and loneliness. Now, she is terrified of going grey and wants to uncover the true effects of ageing. As she embraces growing old for two weeks, Hayley explores what ageing does to people's health, looks and quality of life(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce pushes her body to its limits to explore the rise and impact of the strong look among women. It remains to be seen whether she has what it takes to get muscles in just four weeks, and finds out if it's possible to look strong and sexy(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part one of two. An analysis of her DNA leads Nadiya Hussain to a surprising discovery that sees her travel to far-flung places to explore the people, places and food there. programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up a couples who bought a home in Worcestershire, before revisits one of his buyers who made the move from London to Gloucestershire(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer start their hunt in Stow-on-the-Wold, Gloucestershire, buying items including a military first-aid kit and a toast rack to sell at auction in Cardiff(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: The holiday programme returns, reporting on destinations that can be reached directly from Scotland and the island of Ireland. New presenters Holly Hamilton and JJ Chalmers head to Menorca, where they splash out at a waterpark, take in the views from a cliff-face bar, go lobster fishing, visit the white-washed village of Binibeca and see the famous Menorcan horses in action. In the short break, Eamonn Holmes relives happy childhood memories in the Glens of Antrim(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Bex's concerns for Louise leave Lisa worried and she is more determined than ever to help her daughter. But as Louise opens up, the guilt becomes too much and she makes a shocking confession. Mick forces Linda to face the consequences of her actions, Billy shares his worries about Honey with Lola and Jean enlists Habiba's help to celebrate Kat's birthday. Ben is shocked when Callum arrives at the car lot, while Martin receives good news - the hit-and-run victim is awake(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: For more than a century, the nation's high streets have been key to local communities, but with one in 10 shops sitting empty, they are in crisis. In this programme, business journalist Adam Shaw investigates the government's plans to spend millions of pounds reviving run-down town centres, and finds high streets across the country looking at other ways to remain part of their community. He learns that to adapt and change on the scale that is needed, they need to have more than just money(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a body is found in a field near the village of Hartford, Nikki and the Lyell team confirm it is that of missing teenager Jason Forbes. Working to the theory that most killers strike close to home, ambitious DCI Claire Ashby is committed to proving the guilt of her main suspect and sets her sights firmly on local locksmith Malcolm Wilde. Forensic crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, Richard Lintern, David Caves and Liz Carr, with Sophia Di Martino, Robert Pugh and Phil Davis(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: For more than 30 years, dragon boat racing has been a popular sport among women who have had breast cancer, with the repetitive upper body paddling action believed to help rebuild upper body strength and prevent reoccurrence. This programme follows the members of the Belfast-based crew the Lagan Dragons as they prepare for the annual Dublin Regatta in a bid to retain their title as champions in the breast cancer category(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following Claire and Steve Golemboski-Byrne as they work to build a sustainable, off-grid lifestyle on six acres of farmland in rural Co Down(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer from Bedfordshire goes back to work five months after having her ankle crushed when a suspect deliberately drove his car at her, while bodycam footage reveals the moment a police officer in Halifax has his finger badly bitten as he tries to stop a criminal from spitting at him. A paramedic couple in Coventry face their worst nightmare when the wife hears over the radio that her husband has been attacked and knocked unconscious while attending a patient(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Having previously embarrassing himself in a bar, Liam announces to his girlfriend Jess that he's taking a month off the booze. As he tries to make it through his first sober Friday night in years, he thinks back to where and when his relationship with drinking began as he and his mates tried every trick in the book to buy alcohol(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Comedian, actor and presenter Jason Manford hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by Geordie favourite Chris Ramsey and the multi-talented Doc Brown(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: British actors-turned-Hollywood stars Daniel Radcliffe and Alan Cumming discuss appearing together on the West End stage in Samuel Beckett's tragicomedy Endgame. Actor and writer Sharon Horgan chats about starring in inspirational drama Military Wives, about a band of misfit women who form a choir on a military base, and Miriam Margolyes talks about the new series of Call the Midwife. Plus, Craig David performs a track from his classic album Born to Do It, as he celebrates 20 years since its release, and more intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair try their luck(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville hear from a woman who says she was turned away from a flight with her twin babies because the airline could not cope with them(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large terraced house in Earlsfield in London, ancient woodland in Eythorne in Kent, and a building housing six flats in Tonypandy in Glamorgan are all sold at auction(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: After a bout of ill-health, Harriet Sinfield Day wants to make the rest of her life count. Husband David is willing to do whatever he can to make her happy, but has real concerns about leaving behind their life in the UK. A trial week in Melbourne sees them explore the properties, work opportunities and lifestyle on offer. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Detectives follow a trail of blood that leads to an elusive thief, and fraudsters who conned their victims out of thousands in a home-improvement scam. Dominic Littlewood presents(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Richard Madley lead two teams competing at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, and host Eric Knowles takes time out to view a 19th-century antique from India(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara and Emma are on tenterhooks, while Valerie is concerned about her DNA test results. Daniel gets caught in the middle of two neighbours who are antagonising each other(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Sid returns to Kembleford to ask his old family to help save his new one. Crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah revisits a couple in Cornwall, who used their £350,000 budget to snap up one of the houses she showed them(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to Surrey, where Izzie finds a piece of jewellery that causes quite a stir at auction, and Charlie uncovers a rare table(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Lisa is convinced Louise is unwell after saying what she did about Keanu, and when Sharon gets wind of her stepdaughter's confession, she is convinced it cannot be true. Phil, however, tells a different story. Linda hides her hangover from Mick and feels the pressure at work with Shirley watching her every move, Sonia finds herself in an impossible situation and Billy goes to extreme lengths to help Honey. Rainie tries to help Callum start dating again and Kat is furious to learn Leo sabotaged her plan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Tottenham Hotspur v Middlesbrough (Kick-off 8.05pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the match from Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, as the sides compete for the right to face Southampton away in the next round. Championship outfit Boro took the lead in the reverse fixture through Ashley Fletcher, but Lucas Moura equalised for the Londoners and Jose Mourinho's side will now expect to prevail in front of their own fans(n)

programme.title: Border Land
programme.desc: Rural crime series following law enforcement on both sides of the border(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: A bodybuilder joins the team to help promote the health benefits of a vegan diet, persuading the regulars at local gyms that they do not need animal protein to build up their muscles. The others opt for more aggressive measures by staging a vigil at a slaughterhouse, which leads to a counter-protest from farmers, who feel their livelihood is being threatened(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Asian Odyssey
programme.desc: Part one of two. An analysis of her DNA leads Nadiya Hussain to a surprising discovery that sees her travel to far-flung places to explore the people, places and food there. The former Great British Bake Off winner begins by visiting Cambodia, where she uncovers a bakery school training up girls from tough backgrounds in the art of French patisserie, before heading to Thailand, where she learns how to make a curry paste unique to the Muslim community on the tiny island of Koh Sukhorn(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville ask what the collapse of Thomas Cook may mean for consumers. Plus, the online travel agent whose trips were too good to be true(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin examine the potential of a pair of flats in Clay Cross, Chesterfield, a two-bed apartment in South Norwood, London and a plot of land in Stafford(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman catches up with mum Persephone and partner Debbie, who sampled a week in New Zealand. One year on, cameras reveal whether they decided to make the move(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A prolific thief is taken down after a dramatic bicycle pursuit, and a detective in Wales ends the fifteen year crime campaign of two burglars(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Bear and Karen get more than they bargained for when two patients with complex dilemmas present at evening surgery(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: When a tortured artist is murdered, Father Brown must discover who committed the crime before it is too late. Starring Mark Williams and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson revisits a RAF couple who bought a property in south Wales, before catching up with buyers who found the perfect home for under their £400,000 budget(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: First time farmers Paige and Kingsley are hoping that showing their sheep at the Isle of Wight show will bring them closer to their life dream of becoming full-time farmers(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Charlie Ross and Izzie Balmer head to an auction in Exeter, where they come across a letter rack in an unusual shape, and an Edwardian box containing a surprise(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac and Kian return to work and support each other as they try to conquer their demons, and Sacha's feelings for Essie are only being made worse by living together. Meanwhile, Serena tries to find a new role for herself, but with Jason coping well and a new face to contend with, she may find herself forced out(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Investigating the murder of missing teenager Jason Forbes, Nikki and the Lyell team are forced to question police methods, as DCI Claire Ashby seems more concerned with building the case against her main suspect. With pressure building on the police to secure a conviction, the team must fight to ensure that inconvenient truths are not overlooked(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Paralympian Kadeena Cox and her ex Tes, and Geordie Shore star Sam Gowland and former partner TOWIE's Georgia Harrison, reunite to answer some awkward questions about their relationships. Georgia wants to know if their romance was for real and Kadeena Cox has some burning questions for Tes - but could they get back together?(n)

programme.title: My Mate's A Bad Date
programme.desc: Khalil from Nottingham cannot get a second date, and Oloni thinks that remembering his dates' names may help(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. programme.title: Ceòl bho Perthshire Amber

programme.title: Fonn Fonn Fonn
programme.desc: Quizmaster Gilleasbuig Ferguson, and team captains Ruairidh Munro and Carina Macleod test the musical knowledge, wit and kazoo-playing skills of guests Ricky Hannaway, Alasdair MacCaluim, Rachel Mackay and Iain Macsween. programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie Fowlis and Muireann NicAmhlaoibh explore musical roots in Limerick, accompanied by Pàdruig Morrison and piper Calum Stewart from Morayshire. Amongst other musicians appearing are Deirdre Scanlan, uillean piper Mickey Dunne and Derek Hickey(n)

programme.title: Sulaisgeir
programme.desc: For centuries, men from Ness in the Isle of Lewis in the Western Isles have sailed to a tiny island, 40 miles North of Lewis each summer to collect their harvest of seabirds. Dods MacFarlane has followed in the footsteps of his forefathers for over 40 years, but here he leads the annual guga hunt for the last time(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Tony Blackburn talks about the success of BBC Radio 2's Sounds of the 60s Live, which toured the UK throughout 2019 and a raft of new dates have been added for 2020(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions(n)

programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and topical studio discussion from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David and the team are in Milton Keynes, where a ceramic egg attracts David Ford's attention, Tracy Thackray-Howitt buys a gold cigarette case and Mark Stevens eyes up some Iranian portraits in gold(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five members of a family from Lancashire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants James, Joe, Shelley and Helen pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ryan confronts Graham, Aaron receives troubling news and Jai is feeling the pressure(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz goes back to the police and reveals how she unwittingly bought a gun as part of a house clearance, it disappeared and she panicked. Cathy quizzes Yasmeen about her cleaning obsession, and Aggie confides in Ed that she's considering returning to nursing(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show
programme.desc: It can get pretty tough in January with Christmas bills flooding through the nation's letterboxes, so the financial journalist and Angellica Bell are on hand to help navigate new regulations relating to overdrafts. Plus, there is a look at consumer rights when asking for a refund(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Cathy confides in Brian how she witnessed Geoff being nasty to Yasmeen, and a tearful Fiz concedes that she'll stay in a B&B so the girls can return home. In an attempt to rebuild their marriage, Steve gets down on one knee and asks Tracy to re-marry him(n)

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Adam finds himself in hot water at work, while his relationship with Karen continues to affect the group. Jenny has finished her cancer treatment, but her struggle is far from over, while David begins to see Mary in a new light. The return of Mike Bullen's comedy drama, starring James Nesbitt, Fay Ripley, Hermione Norris and Robert Bathurst(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: Part one of two. The documentary maker goes inside the walls of HMP Belmarsh, the country's most notorious maximum-security jail, which has housed the country's most dangerous - and infamous - convicts, including Ian Huntley, Ronnie Biggs and Charles Bronson. Ross explores how staff cope with high-profile inmates, extremists and common criminals living side-by-side and goes inside the High Security Unit, where he meets Muhammad Asif Hafeez, known as Sultan, the alleged mastermind of a drug-smuggling empire(n)

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Action from the latest edition of the weekly AEW event, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef's journey continues along the Canal du Midi in southern France, starting in Carcassonne, where he joins the tourists taking in the sights and doing a little shopping. After cooking up a delicious fish stew on the banks of the canal, James hops aboard a boat for a trip towards the coast and is treated to a traditional cuttlefish lunch cooked in the boat's tiny kitchen(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Documentary following workers on the Tyne and Wear Metro - the UK's largest metro system outside of London. In the first edition, staff in Sunderland prepare for the arrival of 50,000 Spice Girls fans ahead of the pop group's concert at the Stadium of Light. The pressure rises across the day as head of operations Wayne spots a potential fault with an overhead line, and there's an anxious call to the control room - a set of fans have lost their tickets for the concert of a lifetime. Narrated by Zoe Hakin(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. In the early hours of August 7th 1985, Nevill and June Bamber were shot and killed inside their farmhouse, along with their adoptive daughter, Sheila Caffell, and Sheila's six-year-old twin sons, Daniel and Nicholas Caffell. Police receive a call from the only surviving member of June and Nevill's immediate family, their adoptive son Jeremy. At first, it appears to be an open-and-shut case to investigating officer DCI Thomas 'Taff' Jones, but for DS Stan Jones, something does not feel right(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: The Home Game
programme.desc: A Southampton couple invest £175,000 to modernise their home, while an Australian author plans an enviable loft conversion for her Dartmouth riverfront home. Interior design expert Linda Barker monitors their progress and helps appraise the end results(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron is full of remorse, Jamie blindsides Kim and Andrea, and Jai is on the edge(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: When Brian expresses his concerns for Yasmeen to Geoff, he's quietly seething and makes out that Yasmeen is an alcoholic. David is relieved when the consultant confirms that Shona is slowly emerging from her coma, and Nina warms to Roy when he chats about Hayley(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, the actor and his son head to the Great Smoky Mountains on the Tennessee-North Carolina border, where they go canoeing on the iconic rapids that featured in the film Deliverance. With Bradley left in need of a whisky, they head to a Hillbilly Jam for a nightcap and to experience the mountain tradition of clog dancing, before the pair try their hand at chopping wood with some competitive lumberjacks and taking to the woods to search for a mythical mountain beast(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen listens in disbelief as Peter talks about alcoholism and invites her to join him at one of his meetings. Fiz apologises to David for unwittingly bringing a loaded gun onto the street. Bernie confides in Sean how she's been posing as a 14-year-old boy online to get justice for Paul(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour(n)

programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with MPs, topical guests and cultural figures. Including updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, the actor and his son head to the Great Smoky Mountains on the Tennessee-North Carolina border, where they go canoeing on the iconic rapids that featured in the film Deliverance. With Bradley left in need of a whisky, they head to a Hillbilly Jam for a nightcap and to experience the mountain tradition of clog dancing, before the pair try their hand at chopping wood with some competitive lumberjacks and taking to the woods to search for a mythical mountain beast(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen listens in disbelief as Peter talks about alcoholism and invites her to join him at one of his meetings. programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Stan and his number two Mick Clark speak to Jeremy and relatives of the murdered family, but their picture of what really happened at White House Farm remains inconclusive. The twins' father Colin Caffell is devastated by his loss, and is initially comforted by Jeremy, while post-mortem examinations reveal nothing to contradict the murder-suicide theory. Meanwhile, relative Ann Eaton cannot believe Sheila was responsible, and discusses her growing suspicions with her brother David Boutflour(n)

Paul is also taken by scruffy Yorkshire terrier Titch, who arrives with such long hair he is struggling to move, and a Doberman-cross who was abandoned in Camden. programme.title: Long Lost Family

In this 90-minute special presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, new DNA technology and painstaking detective work is combined to help solve the mysteries of people with unknown beginning. programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The folk of Aidensfield panic when rumours of an escaped killer are confirmed. Rob attempts to track down the fugitive, but his investigations are complicated when Helen gets caught up in the case. David is left reeling when Greengrass's lurcher Alfred becomes seriously ill, and Peggy speaks to bereavement expert Bernie in the hope that he can lend support to the devastated handyman. Rural drama with David Stockwell(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris has his revenge on Lynn, the Sugdens have a visitor from Down Under, Jack and Sarah return from their honeymoon, and there is an unlikely star of the village cricket match(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard decides to bring one association to an end. The Sugdens persuade Sven to extend his stay. Drastic action is called for when there is no reply from the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is fed up that Raquel has gone to Croydon, Denise worries about the salon losing trade, and Maud likes the idea of Maureen retiring as they will spend all day together(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera tries to secure Rita and Sally's silence over what they saw in Blackpool. Reg starts work at Firman's Freezers. Fiona is fed up that there is no work at the salon(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry's latest novel is being published in England - but his siblings do not seem to be very impressed, his mother is more concerned with baking food to sell at the market, and even his attempts to show off in a local bar go unnoticed. Louisa attempts to make it up to him by organising a public reading. Leslie overhears Pavlos explaining to Margo how he makes liquor out of kumquats, and decides to launch a business venture brewing his own - but his sister does not approve of stealing the monk's recipe(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The folk of Aidensfield panic when rumours of an escaped killer are confirmed. Rob attempts to track down the fugitive, but his investigations are complicated when Helen gets caught up in the case. programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle and his team search for a missing boy, their investigation leads them to a less than inviting hotel - and the discovery of the youngster's body in the nearby woods suggests one of the residents could be responsible. Milner finds his loyalty divided when Foyle is placed under house arrest, but the determined investigator vows to prove his innocence. Drama series, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with sidekick Captain Hastings (Hugh Fraser), secretary Miss Lemon (Pauline Moran) and Inspector Japp (Philip Jackson) in a case that plunges him into the world of global espionage as the Second World War looms. The public are in panic after the shocking death of Russian grandmaster Ivan Savaranoff during a game of chess. Poirot must try to determine the good guys from the bad in a complex plot that sees the main figures used like pawns. David Suchet stars in a story adapted by Mark Gatiss and Ian Hallard(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Main suspect Nick goes missing, and Lisa is concerned about Sean's possible involvement in the disappearance. The discovery of a key piece of evidence provokes a number of new theories, and the police begin to look more closely at other members of the Meredith family. Elsewhere, Abbie falls deeper into Vincent's world, bringing Sam with her, while Sean's vigilante justice leads him down a dangerous path. Drama, starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett, Lindsey Coulson and Chanel Cresswell(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The investigation of a burglary at a quarry turns into something far more serious when a security guard's badly beaten body is discovered at the scene of the crime. An autistic teenager is arrested for the murder, despite Inspector Frost's misgivings - a move which triggers a chain of tragic events culminating in a tense stand-off. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Caroline Harker and Maurice Roeves(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: In 1935, beleaguered Bournemouth mother Louisa Durrell decides to uproot her family and build a new life on the sun-drenched island of Corfu. However, with money problems and four unruly children to care for, even a new start might not be enough to banish her troubles. Period drama, starring Keeley Hawes, based on Gerald Durrell's trilogy of Corfu memoirs, which includes My Family and Other Animals(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre returns from Morocco, Ken feels Denise is feeling jealous of his ex, Reg feels fed up trapped in the shop, and Vera is amazed to see how much Tommy has grown(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre helps Mavis out at the Kabin, and shows her holiday photos of Samir to Liz, Rita loses Rosie on the pleasure beach, and Vera agrees to go dancing with Lester(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The captives are close to despair, but the police are closer than they think. Joe bears the burden of responsibility for the situation, while a couple return home to chaos(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police wait patiently as tension at the surrounded Home Farm escalates, and relationships become strained. It remains to be seen whether the kidnappers will give themselves up(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of youths push Peggy down a hole while she's poaching. Lord Ashfordly alerts Miller, who gives chase - but lands himself in trouble when he knocks down an elderly pedestrian. However, the incident gives Peggy a new money-making idea - she'll sue for the 'injury' she sustained in the fall. Meanwhile, Gina is attracted to her handsome college tutor. Tim Brooke-Taylor and Murray Head guest star(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper goes into hospital with suspected appendicitis and George looks forward to some peace and quiet - but Jerry shows up on his doorstep with other ideas. Roy Kinnear guest stars(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. However, the vehicle has similar qualities to its unreliable owner and soon breaks down leaving the duo having to summon help from an unexpected quarter. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullet, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of elderly villains spot a chance for one last, glorious crime spree when the villagers leave Aidensfield en masse to support Rob in a charity football match. Rural police drama, starring Sophie Ward, Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Now that the crisis is over, the media spotlight focuses on the village once more. Kim hesitates in evicting a troublesome tenant, and life for Alan will never be the same(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The burden of guilt weighs heavy on Joe. Emotions run high as friends rally round to help the bereaved. Michael delves deeper into Pollard's murky past(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Don lives a bachelor's life as Ivy goes to a retreat. Deirdre writes a love letter to Samir. Vera is horrified when Rita and the Websters see her kissing Lester(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken spends the day moving his stuff into number 12. Deirdre tells Emily about her holiday romance. Denise gets Fiona to call for an ambulance when she doubles over in pain(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family prepares for a celebration, and Margo is determined to persuade the reclusive Countess Mavrodaki to attend. Sven is put to the test by his potential family, but seems to make a good impression. Gerry begins to question whether keeping animals in his personal zoo is fair, and considers releasing them into the wild(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Sam and Foyle are shot at and almost run over by a speeding vehicle, and discover the incident is part of an illicit fuel racket. An undercover investigation reveals the detective's son Andrew to be involved, and the discovery that a driver at the petrol company has been murdered complicates matters further. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: DS Lisa Armstrong is assigned as the Family Liaison Officer to the case of two missing teenage twins in Morecambe. After meeting the twins' parents Sean and Jess, Lisa is shocked to discover that her connection to the family might be more than just professional. As the complications within the investigation grow, the hunt is on to find the twins before it's too late, but Lisa must also deal with her own family's problems. Six-part crime drama, starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong and Tracie Bennett(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

programme.desc: Des puts his For Sale sign back up, Denise is distraught over the baby, Vera is stunned when Geoff sacks Lester, and Maureen refuses to let Reg out of the shop(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Louisa tries to raise some money by selling homemade food, which attracts the attention of another Englishman. However, a former lover threatens the family's idyllic existence. Gerry comes up with a plan to breed otters, Leslie accidentally shoots Roger, and Margo considers becoming a nun. Return of the drama series based on Gerald Durrell's books, starring Keeley Hawes(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle investigates what he believes to be a burglary at the headquarters of a multinational company, and the murder of a Home Guard member during a large-scale exercise on the Sussex Downs soon leads him to believe the firm are conducting covert business with the Nazis - and that his former police sergeant may be involved. Second World War crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian and chat-show host presents more shaggy dog stories as he meets the canine residents of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London. They include elderly Labrador-cross Hope, who is so distressed by her new surroundings she cowers in the kennels and is initially too scared to be filmed. Paul is also taken by scruffy Yorkshire terrier Titch, who arrives with such long hair he is struggling to move, and a Doberman-cross who was abandoned in Camden. Paul names her Nancy after her streetwise counterpart in Oliver, so she returns the favour by doing what dogs do - all over him(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives and have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. In this 90-minute special presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, new DNA technology and painstaking detective work is combined to help solve the mysteries of people with unknown beginning. The team uses an extraordinary new technique to try to identify birth families and find answers for people who have spent decades striving to find out where they came from(n)

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: With the Meredith family reeling from Dylan's death and Sean's arrest, Lisa is charged with finding his alibi and makes a game-changing discovery. Suspicions are raised about another Morecambe resident's involvement with the twins and the police rush to find them. Abbie grows closer to newcomer Vincent, whilst Rob agrees to take on a risky dare. Frustrated by the police investigation, Sean takes matters into his own hands. Starring Morven Christie, Jonas Armstrong, Tracie Bennett and Lindsey Coulson(n)
